robot_capabilities.burger1: Indoor service robot for fetching items, opening doors, carrying light payloads, and operating in formations.
robot_capabilities.burger2: Indoor service robot for delivery, cooperative transport, and formation-based navigation.
robot_capabilities.burger3: Indoor service robot for interaction with tables, kitchen appliances, and formation tasks.
robot_capabilities.waffle: Heavy-duty mobile robot for carrying larger payloads and assisting in cooperative manipulation tasks.
robot_capabilities.tb4: Autonomous mobile robot for navigation, monitoring, and lightweight transport tasks.
robot_capabilities.go2: Quadruped robot capable of rough terrain traversal, inspection, search, and agile motion.

robot_morphology.burger1: Size: 250 x 250 x 600 mm, Payload: 2 kg, Speed: 0.6 m/s, Degrees of Freedom: 0 (mobile base)
robot_morphology.burger2: Size: 250 x 250 x 600 mm, Payload: 2 kg, Speed: 0.6 m/s, Degrees of Freedom: 0 (mobile base)
robot_morphology.burger3: Size: 250 x 250 x 600 mm, Payload: 2 kg, Speed: 0.6 m/s, Degrees of Freedom: 0 (mobile base)
robot_morphology.waffle: Size: 354 x 354 x 950 mm, Payload: 10 kg, Speed: 0.5 m/s, Degrees of Freedom: 0 (mobile base)
robot_morphology.tb4: Size: 300 x 300 x 800 mm, Payload: 3 kg, Speed: 0.7 m/s, Degrees of Freedom: 0 (mobile base)
robot_morphology.go2: Size: 700 x 430 x 550 mm, Payload: 5 kg, Speed: 0–3.5 m/s, Degrees of Freedom: 12 (leg joints)

task_specific_rules: Burger robots may operate in formation for fetch, surround, escort, and cooperative manipulation tasks.
Waffle may assist with heavy payload transport when assigned jointly with burger robots.
Go2 may be deployed for exploration, fast inspection, and obstacle-rich navigation.
task_replanning_rules: If a robot in formation fails, redistribute formation roles among remaining burger robots.
If go2 encounters unreachable terrain, reassign task to wheeled robots if possible.
If obstacles block appliance access, adjust formation centroid and approach angle.
